---
title: "Your Document Title"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2023-02-22"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
server: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c("rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here",
                       "janitor", "data.table", "lubridate")
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "explore_data.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

```{r}
data <- import(here("data", "processed", 
                    "individual_edm_location.RDATA"), trust = TRUE)
data <- data$yearly
```

## Test Function

```{r}
correlation <- cor(data$total_spill_count, 
                   data$sum_spill_count_yr, 
                   use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x = total_spill_count, y = sum_spill_count_yr)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkblue", alpha = 0.1, size = 1) +  # Scatter points
  geom_abline(slope=1, color = "black", linewidth= 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkred", linewidth= 0.5,
              se = TRUE) +  
  labs(title = "Correlation between Total Spill Count and Sum Spill Count Yr",
       subtitle = paste("Pearson Correlation: ", round(correlation, 2)),
       x = "Total Spill Count",
       y = "Sum Spill Count Yr") +
  theme_minimal() 
```

```{r}
# Basic Enhanced Scatter Plot
# Convert 'water_company' to a factor if it's not already
data$water_company <- as.factor(data$water_company)

# If 'year' is numeric and you prefer it as a factor for discrete shapes
data$year <- as.factor(data$year)

ggplot(data, aes(x = total_spill_count, y = sum_spill_count_yr, 
                         color = water_company, shape = year)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +  # Scatter points with transparency and size
  geom_abline(slope=1, color = "black", linewidth= 0.1) +
  labs(title = "Correlation between Total Spill Count and Sum Spill Count Yr",
       subtitle = "Colored by Water Company and Shaped by Year",
       x = "Total Spill Count",
       y = "Sum Spill Count Yr",
       color = "Water Company",
       shape = "Year") +
  theme_minimal() +  # Clean theme
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )


```

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Spill Count Correlation Analysis"),
  
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput("companies", "Select Water Companies:",
                 choices = unique(data$water_company),
                 multiple = TRUE,
                 selected = unique(data$water_company)),
      
      selectInput("years", "Select Years:",
                 choices = unique(data$year),
                 multiple = TRUE,
                 selected = unique(data$year))
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      plotOutput("correlation_plot")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output) {
  
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    data %>%
      filter(water_company %in% input$companies,
             year %in% input$years)
  })
  
  output$correlation_plot <- renderPlot({
    ggplot(filtered_data(), 
           aes(x = total_spill_count, y = sum_spill_count_yr,
               color = water_company, shape = factor(year))) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, aes(color = water_company)) +
      labs(title = "Correlation between Total Spill Count and Sum Spill Count Yr",
           x = "Total Spill Count",
           y = "Sum Spill Count Yr",
           color = "Water Company",
           shape = "Year") +
      theme_minimal()
  })
}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
```


```{r}
library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Spill Count Correlation Analysis"),
  
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput("companies", "Select Water Companies:",
                 choices = unique(data$water_company),
                 multiple = TRUE,
                 selected = unique(data$water_company)),
      
      selectInput("years", "Select Years:",
                 choices = unique(data$year),
                 multiple = TRUE,
                 selected = unique(data$year))
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      plotlyOutput("correlation_plot")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output) {
  
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    data %>%
      filter(water_company %in% input$companies,
             year %in% input$years)
  })
  
  output$correlation_plot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(filtered_data(), 
                aes(x = total_spill_count, y = sum_spill_count_yr,
                    color = water_company, shape = year,
                    text = paste("Water Company:", water_company,
                               "<br>Year:", year,
                               "<br>Total Spill Count:", total_spill_count,
                               "<br>Sum Spill Count Yr:", sum_spill_count_yr))) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, aes(color = water_company)) +
      labs(title = "Correlation between Total Spill Count and Sum Spill Count Yr",
           x = "Total Spill Count",
           y = "Sum Spill Count Yr",
           color = "Water Company",
           shape = "Year") +
      theme_minimal()
    
    ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
  })
}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
```

```{r}
library(plotly)
library(ggplot2)

# Create base plot with hover text
p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = total_spill_count, y = sum_spill_count_yr, 
                             color = water_company, shape = year,
                             text = paste("Water Company:", water_company,
                                        "<br>Year:", year,
                                        "<br>Total Spill Count:", total_spill_count,
                                        "<br>Sum Spill Count Yr:", sum_spill_count_yr))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, aes(color = water_company)) +
  labs(title = "Correlation between Total Spill Count and Sum Spill Count Yr",
       subtitle = "Colored by Water Company and Shaped by Year",
       x = "Total Spill Count",
       y = "Sum Spill Count Yr",
       color = "Water Company",
       shape = "Year") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# Convert to interactive plotly
interactive_plot <- ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")

# Add additional interactivity features
interactive_plot <- interactive_plot %>%
  layout(
    hoverlabel = list(bgcolor = "white"),
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = '<b>Filter by:</b>'),
      itemsizing = 'constant'
    )
  )

# Save to HTML
htmlwidgets::saveWidget(interactive_plot, "correlation_plot.html")
```

```{r}
initialise_environment()
setup_logging()
data <- load_data()

aggregate_spills <- function(data) {
  # Ensure data.table and copy to avoid modifying input
  dt <- as.data.table(data)
  
  # Add month column
  dt[, month := month(start_time)]
  
  # Clean data and set keys in one step
  dt <- dt[!is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr) & !is.na(start_time)]
  data.table::setkey(dt, water_company, year, month, start_time)
  
  # Create yearly combinations
  yearly_combinations <- expand_grid(
    water_company = unique(dt$water_company),
    year = 2021:2023
  )
  
  # Create monthly combinations
  monthly_combinations <- expand_grid(
    water_company = unique(dt$water_company),
    year = 2021:2023,
    month = 1:12
  )
  
  # Yearly aggregation
  yearly_result <- map2(
    yearly_combinations$water_company,
    yearly_combinations$year,
    ~{
      # Get data for current company-year
      current_data <- dt[water_company == .x & year == .y]
      
      if (nrow(current_data) == 0) {
        return(data.table(
          water_company = .x,
          year = .y,
          total_spill_count = 0L,
          total_spill_hrs = 0,
          sum_spill_count_yr = 0,
          sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr = 0
        ))
      }
      
      # Get unique keys for current subset
      keys_year <- unique(c(unlist(current_data$key_join), 
                            "outlet_discharge_ngr"))
      
      # Group by keys and calculate statistics
      current_data[, .(
        total_spill_count = count_spills(start_time, end_time),
        total_spill_hrs = sum(as.numeric(difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "hours")), na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_spill_count_yr = mean(sum_spill_count_yr, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr = mean(sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr, na.rm = TRUE)
      ), by = keys_year]
    }
  )
  
  # Monthly aggregation
  monthly_result <- pmap(
    list(
      monthly_combinations$water_company,
      monthly_combinations$year,
      monthly_combinations$month
    ),
    ~{
      # Get data for current company-year-month
      current_data <- dt[water_company == ..1 & 
                           year == ..2 & 
                           month == ..3]
      
      if (nrow(current_data) == 0) {
        return(data.table(
          water_company = ..1,
          year = ..2,
          month = ..3,
          total_spill_count = 0L,
          total_spill_hrs = 0,
          sum_spill_count_yr = 0,
          sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr = 0
        ))
      }
      
      # Get unique keys for current subset
      keys_month <- unique(c(unlist(current_data$key_join), 
                             "outlet_discharge_ngr"))
      
      # Group by keys and calculate statistics
      current_data[, .(
        total_spill_count = count_spills(start_time, end_time),
        total_spill_hrs = sum(as.numeric(difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "hours")), na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_spill_count_yr = mean(sum_spill_count_yr, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr = mean(sum_spill_duration_hrs_yr, na.rm = TRUE)
      ), by = keys_month]
    }
  )
  
  return(list(
    yearly = bind_rows(yearly_result),
    monthly = bind_rows(monthly_result)
  ))
}
```

```{r}
monthly_result
```
